headline: The Economy Cabin on Airplanes Keeps on Shrinking
summary: Airlines are retrofitting their passenger jets or buying new ones that have a larger share of premium seats.  Carriers with first-class sections like  Delta Air Lines  and  United Airlines  have increased premium seating on flights over the past decade.  Now, low-frills flying pioneer  Southwest Airlines  and low-cost carriers like Spirit and Frontier are adding seats that give passengers perks like a few extra inches of leg space.

content.title: The Economy Cabin on Airplanes Keeps on Shrinking
content.summary: Airlines are retrofitting their passenger jets or buying new ones that have a larger share of premium seats.  Carriers with first-class sections like  Delta Air Lines  and  United Airlines  have increased premium seating on flights over the past decade.  Now, low-frills flying pioneer  Southwest Airlines  and low-cost carriers like Spirit and Frontier are adding seats that give passengers perks like a few extra inches of leg space.
